issue: `validateAndTransitionVault(deps.vaultId, input.userSelectedPath, now)` is called and its return value is discarded. Because the helper is pure (no I/O, no shared mutable state), the call has no observable effect on the pipeline output: the event built on lines 65-70 is constructed independently from `vaultId`, `userSelectedPath`, and `now`. The line is dead code in production. This contradicts the `Step 4` comment that calls it an `internal invariant check` — there is no check, the helper never throws and never refuses any input. Removing line 62 would not change a single test outcome.
remediation: Either delete line 62 (and update the step comment) and rely on `validate-and-transition.test.ts` to cover the helper, or surface the produced `Vault` in the pipeline (e.g., return it alongside the event, or persist it via a new port) so the call carries weight. Document the choice in REQ-008/REQ-014.
